div.p: Three approaches to coroutine stack management

Segmented stacks caused "hot split" problem: frequently-called functions that barely exceeded chunk size caused constant allocation/deallocation. Contiguous stacks with mremap grow once, shrink later, and maintain cache locality. The cost of copying during growth is amortized. Your choice: Fixed-size (64 KB) is simplest for learning. Contiguous is production-ready but requires OS support.

g.text: Fixed-Size | Wasteful (64KB each) | Simple | Good | Overflow crashes
Segmented | Efficient | Complex | Poor (pointer chase) | Fragmentation
Contiguous | Efficient | Moderate | Excellent | Relocation cost
